claim_boundary: This preflight is read-only. It does not push, merge, publish, or mutate GitHub. A remote update still requires explicit human R4 approval.

commands.post_push_verify: git fetch origin && git rev-parse HEAD origin/codex/seed-pr-ci-source-evidence origin/main

pending_remote_updates.rollback: restore feature to previous remote SHA 44b3338c55c64593cc18ea2783c3dc664fbe4cf8 with an approved restore action

pending_remote_updates.action: fast-forward push current HEAD to main
pending_remote_updates.rollback: restore main to previous remote SHA 44b3338c55c64593cc18ea2783c3dc664fbe4cf8 with an approved revert/restore action

r4_disclosure.action: push current HEAD to feature; fast-forward push current HEAD to main
r4_disclosure.impact: GitHub pending refs become externally visible at the local development state.
r4_disclosure.risk: Main CI and external reviewers immediately see the current commits.
r4_disclosure.rollback: restore feature to previous remote SHA 44b3338c55c64593cc18ea2783c3dc664fbe4cf8 with an approved restore action; restore main to previous remote SHA 44b3338c55c64593cc18ea2783c3dc664fbe4cf8 with an approved revert/restore action
r4_disclosure.verification: fetch origin and compare remote feature/main refs with local HEAD after push

receipt_commit_boundary.claim_boundary: This receipt records GitHub sync state at source_commit_sha. A later commit that only refreshes productization/surface evidence can remain fresh in the PM gate as an evidence-only delta; any source-code or non-evidence delta must regenerate this preflight before claiming GitHub sync release credit.
